paragraphs.source: NVIDIA introduced distributed shared memory (DSMEM) in the Hopper architecture. DSMEM allows CTAs within a cluster to access each otherРђЎs shared memory. This is useful for patterns like inter-CTA reductions. Reading peer-CTA memory through DSMEM has significantly lower throughput than SMEMРђЎs 128 bytes per clock cycle.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: NVIDIA introduced distributed shared memory (DSMEM) in the Hopper architecture. DSMEM allows CTAs within a cluster to access each otherРђЎs shared memory. This is useful for patterns like inter-CTA reductions. Reading peer-CTA memory through DSMEM has significantly lower throughput than SMEMРђЎs 128 bytes per clock cycle.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: NVIDIA introduced distributed shared memory (DSMEM) in the Hopper architecture. DSMEM allows CTAs within a cluster to access each otherРђЎs shared memory. This is useful for patterns like inter-CTA reductions. Reading peer-CTA memory through DSMEM has significantly lower throughput than SMEMРђЎs 128 bytes per clock cycle.

pro.text: УІ▒С╝ЪУЙЙтюе Hopper ТъХТъёСИГт╝ЋтЁЦС║єтѕєтИЃт╝ЈтЁ▒С║ФтєЁтГў (DSMEM)сђѓDSMEM тЁЂУ«ИСИђСИфжЏєуЙцтєЁуџётЇЈСйюу║┐уеІжўхтѕЌ (CTA) У«┐жЌ«тй╝ТГцуџётЁ▒С║ФтєЁтГў (SMEM)сђѓУ┐Ўт»╣С║ј CTAжЌ┤тйњу║дуГЅТеАт╝ЈжЮътИИТюЅућесђѓжђџУ┐Є DSMEM У»╗тЈќт»╣уГЅ CTA тєЁтГўуџётљътљљжЄЈ№╝їТўЙУЉЌСйјС║јтЁ▒С║ФтєЁтГў (SMEM) Т»ЈТЌХжњЪтЉеТюЪ 128 тГЌУіѓуџётљътљљжЄЈсђѓ

key_points: DSMEM enables inter-CTA reductions but operates at a significantly lower throughput than local SMEM.
Compiler emission choices (LDS vs. generic LD) heavily impact the ability to achieve peak throughput, requiring workarounds like UBLKCP.

issues.description: У»ЉТќЄТюфжЂхтЙф Guidelines СИГтЁ│С║јРђют░є PTX тњї SASS ТїЄС╗цТа╝т╝ЈтїќСИ║тєЁУЂћС╗БуаЂ (inline code)РђЮуџёт╗║У««сђѓУЎйуёХу┐╗У»ЉТюгУ║ФтЄєуА«№╝їСйєу╝║т░ЉтЈЇт╝ЋтЈитїЁУБ╣С╝џжЎЇСйјТіђТю»ТќЄТАБуџёУДёУїЃТђДтњїтЈ»У»╗ТђДсђѓ

paragraphs.source: We experimented with a few different PTX patterns for interacting with DSMEM. An important difference when writing code for DSMEM vs. SMEM is that DSMEM loads are packetized similar to global loads, so the optimal access pattern looks nothing like the interleaved accesses that avoid bank conflicts in local SMEM, and more like a typical coalesced access to contiguous locations in GMEM. Additionally, we observed that in order to get the full 128B/cycle for local SMEM, ld.shared must be used without ::cluster. This was a pitfall we ran into when we wrote a benchmark which simply used ld.shared::cluster to local and remote SMEM addresses.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: We experimented with a few different PTX patterns for interacting with DSMEM. An important difference when writing code for DSMEM vs. SMEM is that DSMEM loads are packetized similar to global loads, so the optimal access pattern looks nothing like the interleaved accesses that avoid bank conflicts in local SMEM, and more like a typical coalesced access to contiguous locations in GMEM. Additionally, we observed that in order to get the full 128B/cycle for local SMEM, `ld.shared` must be used without `::cluster`. This was a pitfall we ran into when we wrote a benchmark which simply used `ld.shared::cluster` to local and remote SMEM addresses. With `ld.shared`, the compiler emits `LDS` instead of a generic `LD` emitted with `ld.shared::cluster` which does not seem to be able to achieve peak throughput for local SMEM. We also struggled to push the achieved throughput further with `ld.shared::cluster`, and only achieved slightly higher throughput through DSMEM after switching to `cp.async.bulk`(PTX) / `UBLKCP`(SASS) to move higher volume of data per instruction.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: We experimented with a few different PTX patterns for interacting with DSMEM. An important difference when writing code for DSMEM vs. SMEM is that DSMEM loads are packetized similar to global loads, so the optimal access pattern looks nothing like the interleaved accesses that avoid bank conflicts in local SMEM, and more like a typical coalesced access to contiguous locations in GMEM. Additionally, we observed that in order to get the full 128B/cycle for local SMEM, ld.shared must be used without ::cluster. This was a pitfall we ran into when we wrote a benchmark which simply used ld.shared::cluster to local and remote SMEM addresses. With ld.shared, the compiler emits LDS instead of a generic LD emitted with ld.shared::cluster which does not seem to be able to achieve peak throughput for local SMEM. We also struggled to push the achieved throughput further with ld.shared::cluster, and only achieved slightly higher throughput through DSMEM after switching to cp.async.bulk(PTX) / UBLKCP(SASS) to move higher volume of data per instruction.

pro.text: ТѕЉС╗гТхІУ»ЋС║єтЄауДЇСИј DSMEM С║цС║њуџёСИЇтљї PTX ТеАт╝ЈсђѓСИ║ DSMEM у╝ќтєЎС╗БуаЂСИјСИ║тЁ▒С║ФтєЁтГў (SMEM) у╝ќтєЎС╗БуаЂуџёСИђСИфжЄЇУдЂтї║тѕФтюеС║ј№╝їDSMEM тіаУййСИјтЁет▒ђтіаУййу▒╗С╝╝№╝їжЄЄућеТЋ░ТЇ«тїЁтїќС╝аУЙЊ№╝ЏтЏаТГц№╝їтЁХТюђС╝ўУ«┐жЌ«ТеАт╝Јт«їтЁеСИЇтљїС║јТюгтю░тЁ▒С║ФтєЁтГў (SMEM) СИГжЂ┐тЁЇ bank тє▓уфЂуџёС║цжћЎУ«┐жЌ«№╝їУђїТЏ┤тЃЈтЁет▒ђтєЁтГў (GMEM) СИГт»╣У┐ъу╗Гтю░тЮђуџётЁИтъІтљѕт╣ХУ«┐жЌ«сђѓТГцтцќТѕЉС╗гУДѓт»Ътѕ░№╝їСИ║С║єтюеТюгтю░тЁ▒С║ФтєЁтГў (SMEM) СИГУјитЙЌт«їТЋ┤уџё 128 тГЌУіѓ/тЉеТюЪтљътљљжЄЈ№╝їт┐ЁжА╗Сй┐уће ld.shared УђїСИЇтИд ::cluster С┐«жЦ░угдсђѓТѕЉС╗гтюеу╝ќтєЎтЪ║тЄєТхІУ»ЋТЌХТЏЙжЎитЁЦСИђСИфУ»»тї║№╝їтЇ│т»╣Тюгтю░тњїУ┐юуеІтЁ▒С║ФтєЁтГў (SMEM) тю░тЮђжЃйу«ђтЇЋтю░Сй┐ућеС║є ld.shared::clusterсђѓ

pro.text: Сй┐уће ld.shared ТЌХ№╝їу╝ќУ»ЉтЎеС╝џућЪТѕљ LDS ТїЄС╗ц№╝їУђїСИЇТў»тЃЈСй┐уће ld.shared::cluster ТЌХжѓБТаиућЪТѕљжђџућеуџё LD ТїЄС╗ц№╝їтљјУђЁС╝╝С╣јТЌаТ│Ћт«ъуј░Тюгтю░тЁ▒С║ФтєЁтГў (SMEM) уџёт│░тђ╝тљътљљжЄЈсђѓТѕЉС╗гС╣ЪтЈЉуј░тЙѕжџЙУ┐ЏСИђТГЦТЈљтЇЄ ld.shared::cluster уџёт«ъжЎЁтљътљљжЄЈ№╝їуЏ┤тѕ░тѕЄТЇбСИ║Сй┐уће cp.async.bulk(PTX) / UBLKCP(SASS) ТЮЦУ«ЕТ»ЈТЮАТїЄС╗цТљгУ┐љТЏ┤тцДжЄЈуџёТЋ░ТЇ«тљј№╝їТЅЇтюе DSMEM СИіт«ъуј░С║єуЋЦжФўСИђуѓ╣уџётљътљљжЄЈсђѓ
